title: AddingInterventionNodes

lean_statement.name: subset_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J_union_V_of_subset_swig
lean_statement.code:
-- helper required to state the row
/-- LHS-iterate helper: if `W₂ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V`, then `Sum.inl '' W₂` is
contained in the node-set union of `G.swig W₁ hW₁`. Discharges the
outer-`ext` precondition for
`((G.swig W₁ hW₁).extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes (Sum.inl '' W₂) _)`
on the LHS of claim_3_15. -/
theorem subset_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J_union_V_of_subset_swig
    {G : CDMG α} {W₁ W₂ : Set α} (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.V)
    (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V) :
    Sum.inl '' W₂ ⊆
      (G.swig W₁ hW₁).J ∪ (G.swig W₁ hW₁).V := by

lean_statement.name: subset_swig_V_of_subset_V_of_ext
lean_statement.code:
-- helper required to state the row
/-- RHS-iterate helper: if `W₁ ⊆ G.V`, then `Sum.inl '' W₁` is
contained in the vertex set of
`G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W₂ hW₂`. Discharges the outer-
`swig` precondition for
`((G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W₂ hW₂).swig (Sum.inl '' W₁) _)`
on the RHS of claim_3_15. -/
theorem subset_swig_V_of_subset_V_of_ext
    {G : CDMG α} {W₁ W₂ : Set α} (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.V)
    (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V) :
    Sum.inl '' W₁ ⊆ (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W₂ hW₂).V := by

lean_statement.name: swig_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_comm_equiv
lean_statement.code:
/-- claim_3_15 (`AddingInterventionNodes`): for a CDMG `G : CDMG α`
and disjoint subsets `W₁ ⊆ G.V`, `W₂ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V`, the SWIG on `W₁`
and the intervention-node extension on `W₂` commute. Mirrors the
displayed equation in the `\Lem` at
`lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` line 881.

The body composes a 5-step carrier equiv (`swigExtCarrierEquiv`) with
a local `setCongr` bridge, then closes J/V/E/L componentwise --
mirroring the LN's four field-equality arguments at `graphs.tex`
lines 887 -- 930. The carrier transport rests on the LN's identity
`W₂ \ Ĵ = W₂ \ J` (line 916) -- because the inner SWIG's "extra"
input layer `W₁^i = Set.range Sum.inr` is constructor-disjoint from
`Sum.inl '' W₂`, the diff-from-`Ĵ` and diff-from-`J` versions of the
fresh-extension index set coincide. -/
noncomputable def swig_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_comm_equiv
    {G : CDMG α} {W₁ W₂ : Set α}
    (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.V) (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V) (_hdisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    CDMGEquiv
      ((G.swig W₁ hW₁).extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes
          (Sum.inl '' W₂)
          (subset_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J_union_V_of_subset_swig hW₁ hW₂))
      ((G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W₂ hW₂).swig
          (Sum.inl '' W₁)
          (subset_swig_V_of_subset_V_of_ext hW₁ hW₂)) := by

lean_explanation:
The LN states the equality of two CDMGs $(G_{\swig(W_1)})_{\doit(I_{W_2})} = (G_{\doit(I_{W_2})})_{\swig(W_1)}$ between two ways of composing a SWIG (node-splitting hard intervention) and an intervention-node extension. The Lean encoding works over `α : Type u`, the ambient vertex type matching the LN's silent assumption that $G$ lives over some carrier; `G : CDMG α` is the LN's $G = (J, V, E, L)$; `W₁ W₂ : Set α` are the two subsets. The hypotheses `hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.V` and `hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V` are the LN's $W_1 \subseteq V$ and $W_2 \subseteq J \cup V$, mirroring the LN's own asymmetry (SWIG demands $W \subseteq V$, extension accepts $W \subseteq J \cup V$); `_hdisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂` is the LN's disjointness, encoded via Mathlib's order-theoretic `Disjoint`, which on `Set` unfolds to $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$.

The LN's $G_{\swig(W_1)}$ is `G.swig W₁ hW₁` (def_3_12, a `noncomputable abbrev` over `nodeSplittingHardInterventionOn`), which extends the carrier to `α ⊕ ↑W₁` by splitting each $w \in W_1$ into an observation copy ($\mathrm{Sum.inl}\,w$) and a fresh input copy (in `Set.range Sum.inr`). The LN's $G_{\doit(I_{W_2})}$ is `G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W₂ hW₂` (def_3_13), which mints a fresh intervention input $I_w$ for each $w \in W_2 \setminus J$ and rebases the carrier to `α ⊕ ↑(W₂ \ G.J)`. Because *both* operators are carrier-changing, the two iterates live over different — not even definitionally equal — carriers (`(α ⊕ ↑W₁) ⊕ ↑((Sum.inl '' W₂) \ (G.swig W₁ hW₁).J)` on the LHS vs `(α ⊕ ↑(W₂ \ G.J)) ⊕ ↑(Sum.inl '' W₁)` on the RHS, disagreeing on every factor of the nested `Sum`), so the LN's `=` ships as `CDMGEquiv`: a carrier-rewriting `α ≃ β` plus four image-equality fields for $J$, $V$, $E$, $L$ (the structure is imported from `TwoDisjointNodeSplittingsCommute.lean`).

Mathlib idioms worth flagging: `Sum.inl '' W` is `Set.image` (the canonical embedding of a `Set α` under `Sum.inl : α → α ⊕ X` for whichever `X` the inner operator contributes); subtypes `↑W` (coerced `{x // x ∈ W}`) carry the LN's output / fresh-input indices inside the `Sum`; `Equiv.sumAssoc`, `Equiv.sumComm`, `Equiv.Set.image`, and `Equiv.setCongr` compose the carrier-level bridge (`swigExtCarrierEquiv` chains five of these, and the headline body adds a `setCongr` step that absorbs the LN's $W_2 \setminus \hat J = W_2 \setminus J$ identity).

The row exposes one `CDMGEquiv`-valued declaration (the headline `swig_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_comm_equiv`, implicit `{G W₁ W₂}` and explicit `hW₁ hW₂ _hdisj`) plus two named subset helpers that discharge the iterated operators' type-level preconditions.
design_choices:
**`CDMGEquiv` rather than literal `Eq` — forced by typing, not stylistic.** Both operators are carrier-changing, and the two iterates in the conclusion live over different carrier types: the LHS iterate's carrier is `(α ⊕ ↑W₁) ⊕ ↑((Sum.inl '' W₂) \ (G.swig W₁ hW₁).J)` (swig contributes `α ⊕ ↑W₁`, the outer extension subtypes its `Sum.inr` summand directly), while the RHS iterate's carrier is `(α ⊕ ↑(W₂ \ G.J)) ⊕ ↑(Sum.inl '' W₁)` (extension contributes `α ⊕ ↑(W₂ \ G.J)`, the outer swig subtypes its `Sum.inr` summand by `Sum.inl '' W₁`). These are not even def-equal — they disagree on every factor of the nested `Sum`, including a `\setminus J`-style diff that exists on the LHS but not the RHS. The LN's $W_2 \setminus \hat J = W_2 \setminus J$ identity holds as a set equation in the post-swig carrier under constructor-disjointness, but not as a predicate on `α ⊕ ↑W₁`, so literal `Eq` is not type-correct even with `Disjoint W₁ W₂` in scope. `CDMGEquiv` (with its `refl`/`symm`/`trans` groupoid laws) is the categorified version: a carrier-rewriting `Equiv` plus four image-equality fields for $J$, $V$, $E$, $L$. Contrast with claim_3_11 (`HardInterventionSwigCommute.lean`), which iterates a *carrier-preserving* hard intervention with a SWIG and lands in literal `Eq`; here both legs are carrier-changing, making this the most carrier-asymmetric commute identity in Section 3.2.

**The LN's $W_2 \setminus \hat J = W_2 \setminus J$ identity becomes a `setCongr` bridge in the carrier transport.** The outer LHS iterate's `Sum.inr` subtype is indexed by `(Sum.inl '' W₂) \ (G.swig W₁ hW₁).J`. Unfolding `(G.swig W₁ hW₁).J = Sum.inl '' G.J ∪ Set.range Sum.inr`, the constructor-disjointness fact `Sum.inl ≠ Sum.inr` collapses the diff against `Set.range Sum.inr` to vacuous (a `Sum.inl w` can never match `Sum.inr ⟨_, _⟩`), leaving only the diff against `Sum.inl '' G.J`. So the LHS subtype equals `Sum.inl '' (W₂ \ G.J)` as a set over `α ⊕ ↑W₁` — the carrier-level transcription of the LN's $W_2 \setminus \hat J = W_2 \setminus J$ identity at `graphs.tex` line 916, where $\hat J = J \cup W_1^i$ is the SWIG's input set. In the LN the proof argues "$w \in W_1^i$" is impossible because $W_1^i$ is a set of fresh labels disjoint from any original vertex; in Lean the same "impossible" is `Sum.inl ≠ Sum.inr`. The proof exposes this via a local `h_bridge` set-equation lemma; an `Equiv.setCongr h_bridge` step then absorbs the predicate-level relabel before the 5-step `swigExtCarrierEquiv` composition does the rest of the carrier transport.

**No fusion-plus-commute split — claim_3_15 has only the commute identity.** Unlike claim_3_10 (`TwoDisjointSwigsCommute.lean`) or claim_3_14 chain 1 (`AddingInterventionNodesCommute.lean`), which iterate the *same* carrier-changing operator on two different sets and expose both a fusion lemma (collapsing the iterate into a single operator-call on $W_1 \cup W_2$) and a commute corollary, claim_3_15 iterates *two different* carrier-changing operators (SWIG and intervention-node extension) on *two different* sets ($W_1$ and $W_2$). There is no "joint third term" — no single operator on $\alpha$ that the iterated SWIG-then-`ext` or `ext`-then-SWIG would both reduce to — so the LN itself states only the commute identity and we expose only `_comm_equiv`.

**Named subset helpers, not inlined preconditions.** Each iterate's outer operator requires a subset precondition that the LN swallows silently. The LHS outer `ext` demands `Sum.inl '' W₂ ⊆ (G.swig W₁ hW₁).J ∪ (G.swig W₁ hW₁).V`, and the RHS outer `swig` demands `Sum.inl '' W₁ ⊆ (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W₂ hW₂).V`. Discharging these inline as tactic blocks inside the headline signature would drown the `CDMGEquiv`-valued conclusion and force every consumer to re-prove the same subset facts under any goal-state perturbation. The two named theorems `subset_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J_union_V_of_subset_swig` (LHS) and `subset_swig_V_of_subset_V_of_ext` (RHS) factor the bookkeeping out once. Neither helper needs `Disjoint W₁ W₂` — the embedding layer is insensitive to how $W_1$ and $W_2$ overlap; disjointness is load-bearing only inside the headline `CDMGEquiv`'s carrier-level bridge. Same factoring convention as the sibling helpers in claim_3_14 (chain 1, chain 2) and claim_3_10.

**`Sum.inl ''`-lifts on the inner-set arguments of both sides — forced by carrier-typing.** Each side's outer operator acts on the carrier of its inner CDMG. The LHS outer `ext` acts on `G.swig W₁ hW₁` (carrier `α ⊕ ↑W₁`), so its target set must be a `Set (α ⊕ ↑W₁)`, not `Set α`; the LN-faithful lift is `Sum.inl '' W₂`. Symmetrically, the RHS outer `swig` acts on `G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W₂ hW₂` (carrier `α ⊕ ↑(W₂ \ G.J)`), so its target set is `Sum.inl '' W₁`. The convention `Sum.inl = original-α-side` is inherited from `NodeSplittingOn.lean` and `ExtendingCDMGsWithInterventionNodes.lean`; the LN's "the same $W_2$" / "the same $W_1$" *is* the `Sum.inl`-image of the original set under the implicit identification $\alpha \cong \mathrm{Sum.inl}\,''\,\alpha$ that the LN makes silently but Lean cannot. Same lift / same justification as `hardInterventionOn_swig_comm` in claim_3_11 and the chain-1 / chain-2 lifts in claim_3_14.

**Hypothesis `W₁ ⊆ G.V` is stricter than `W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V` — the LN says so.** SWIG (def_3_12) is restricted to $W \subseteq V$ in the LN, whereas extension (def_3_13) accepts $W \subseteq J \cup V$. The hypothesis asymmetry `W₁ ⊆ G.V` vs `W₂ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V` is the LN's own (not a Lean-only restriction); keeping it LN-tight lets the RHS-helper `subset_swig_V_of_subset_V_of_ext` close by a one-line `Set.image_mono` and matches the LN hypothesis shape verbatim. Weakening to `W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V` and adding a side-condition was considered but rejected — it would diverge from the LN.

**Foundation reuse: `CDMGEquiv` stays in `TwoDisjointNodeSplittingsCommute.lean`.** This row is the fourth in-Section-3.2 consumer of the `CDMGEquiv` structure (after claim_3_7, claim_3_10, claim_3_14). A fourth consumer was considered as the natural "promote to a shared Section-3.1 foundation file" trigger but rejected: the import cost is a single line per consumer, no fifth consumer with a pinned Lean shape is queued in Section 3.2, and the promotion will be revisited if a chapter-4 (CBN-side) row demands the same shape.
